frameworks.name: activity-dependent synaptic plasticity
frameworks.one_line: LTP induction modulated by weak extracellular electric fields

frameworks.name: spaced learning
frameworks.one_line: repeated spaced pairings accumulate plasticity beyond single-shot induction

core_question: Does pairing weak DCS with spaced LTP induction protocols amplify the small in-vitro plasticity effect to a magnitude consistent with behavioral tDCS effects?
key_assumptions: DCS-generated electric fields modulate activity-dependent plasticity
in-vitro plasticity is a mechanistic substrate for tDCS behavioral effects
spacing of induction events can compound plasticity changes
manipulations: presence vs absence of weak DCS during induction
key_findings: weak DCS paired with spaced LTP induction yields cumulative plasticity larger than either alone
the cumulative boost scales with the number of pairings
interpretations: spacing amplifies the weak DCS plasticity effect to a size that can plausibly underlie tDCS behavioral memory effects
